channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: With a Round of 32 spot already clinched, the U.S. takes on Turkey in the World Cup
item.description: Two U.S. wins and two Turkey losses already on the books mean the Americans have won this World Cup group no matter the outcome of Thursday's game. Still, the Americans say they're playing to win.
item.content:encoded:
Two U.S. wins and two Turkey losses already on the books mean the Americans have won this World Cup group no matter the outcome of Thursday's game. Still, the Americans say they're playing to win.

(Image credit: Richard Heathcote)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: At least 32 killed, 700 injured in 2 major earthquakes in Venezuela, says acting president
item.description: A 7.2-magnitude earthquake and a 7.5-magnitude were less than a minute apart, said the U.S. Geological Survey. The second earthquake was the largest to hit the country since 1900.
item.content:encoded:
A 7.2-magnitude earthquake and a 7.5-magnitude were less than a minute apart, said the U.S. Geological Survey. The second earthquake was the largest to hit the country since 1900.

(Image credit: Juan Barreto)
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman

item.title: Meta plans to release AI-powered prediction market app, documents show
item.description: The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."
item.content:encoded:
The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."

(Image credit: Kelly Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: A judge says the Kennedy Center must update him on its plans — and address that tarp
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Kennedy Center to update him on programming and operational plans. But with most of the staff gone and many artists booked elsewhere, what shows would they present?
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has ordered the Kennedy Center to update him on programming and operational plans. But with most of the staff gone and many artists booked elsewhere, what shows would they present?

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Postal Service says its cash crisis is delayed until at least 2031, but problems loom
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service is no longer set to be out of cash in 2027, the agency's head says. But its finances remain shaky as Trump officials keep putting it in political hot water.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Postal Service is no longer set to be out of cash in 2027, the agency's head says. But its finances remain shaky as Trump officials keep putting it in political hot water.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Will Texas' new top voting official be a 'disruptor'? Locals are preparing for it
item.description: Just ahead of closely contested midterms, Texas is about to get a new top voting official. Many locals there fear the frontrunner is a state lawmaker and pastor with no election experience.
item.content:encoded:
Just ahead of closely contested midterms, Texas is about to get a new top voting official. Many locals there fear the frontrunner is a state lawmaker and pastor with no election experience.

(Image credit: Tony Gutierrez)
item.dc:creator: Miles Parks

item.title: Understanding 'masculinism,' a movement to restore the primacy of men
item.description: Masculinism is a belief that feminism emasculates men, and men should be in control while women stay at home raising children. The Atlantic writer Helen Lewis says the movement is becoming mainstream.
item.content:encoded: Masculinism is a belief that feminism emasculates men, and men should be in control while women stay at home raising children. The Atlantic writer Helen Lewis says the movement is becoming mainstream.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: US eases restriction on Iran's World Cup team, allowing travel 2 days before next match
item.description: The U.S. is easing its restrictions on Iran's World Cup team. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security said Tuesday the squad could travel into the country two days before its next match.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. is easing its restrictions on Iran's World Cup team. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security said Tuesday the squad could travel into the country two days before its next match.

(Image credit: Gregory Bull)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Star Fox Review: Can't quite teach an old Fox new tricks
item.description: The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.
item.content:encoded:
The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.

(Image credit: James Mastromarino/Nintendo)
item.dc:creator: James Perkins Mastromarino

item.title: Greetings from sweltering Switzerland
item.description: On the waterfront in Lucerne, Switzerland, soccer fans watched jumbo TVs showing a World Cup match played an ocean away. But the air felt more like the tropics.
item.content:encoded: On the waterfront in Lucerne, Switzerland, soccer fans watched jumbo TVs showing a World Cup match played an ocean away. But the air felt more like the tropics.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz
